---
title: "Using scCCESS"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Using scCCESS}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, eval = FALSE}
library(scCCESS)
library(SingleCellExperiment)

data("sce", package = "scCCESS")
dat <- SingleCellExperiment::counts(sce)

dat <- prefilter(dat)

k_result <- estimate_k(
  dat,
  seed = 1,
  cluster_func = function(x, centers) {
    set.seed(42)
    kmeans(x, centers)
  },
  criteria_method = "NMI",
  krange = 5:15,
  ensemble_sizes = 10,
  cores = 1
)

clusters <- ensemble_cluster(
  dat,
  seed = 1,
  cluster_func = function(x) {
    set.seed(1)
    kmeans(x, centers = k_result$ngroups)
  },
  genes_as_rows = TRUE,
  ensemble_sizes = 10,
  cores = 1,
  verbose = 0,
  scale = FALSE,
  batch_size = 64
)
```

The example uses `eval = FALSE` because scCCESS trains autoencoders through
Keras/TensorFlow. Runtime depends on the configured TensorFlow backend and
available CPU/GPU resources.
